source_notes: This reference describes the simulator's current implemented interpretation, not raw game tooltip text.
Runtime behavior is controlled by data/actions.json, data/buffs.json, data/weapons.json, party presets, transition config, and mechanics modules.

scope.title: Aemeath Implemented Scope
included: global time stop for source-backed liberation and finale actions
Aemeath Resonance Mode: tune_rupture, fusion_burst, and unresolved fallback
Tune Rupture - Shifting / Fusion Burst mechanic event emission
Everbright Polestar runtime weapon passive effects
Tune Rupture Response - Starburst off-field party response
Aemeath C0 Rupturous Trail source-confirmed target mechanic
Seraphic Duet source-confirmed Tune Rupture follow-up damage and target-stack multiplier
QTE / Intro transition metadata and reviewed transition action candidates
excluded: exact hitstop / enemy movement / hit loss
sequence/chain variants not represented in action data

resources.name: Synchronization Rate
modeled_behavior: Gained from Basic attacks, Sync Strike, Form Switch A1/A2 equivalents, and Overdrive +30.
Seraphic Duet costs 100 Synchronization Rate.
Finale clears Synchronization Rate.

resources.name: Resonance Rate
modeled_behavior: Overdrive grants +1 Resonance Rate.
Seraphic Duet grants +1 Resonance Rate.
Finale requires 4 Resonance Rate and clears it.

resources.name: Resonance Energy
modeled_behavior: Starts full in the current Nightmare/Matrix context.
Overdrive costs 125 when the resource system is active.

resources.name: Concerto
modeled_behavior: Preserved when present in action data.
Used by transition systems where configured.

states.name: Seraphic Duo
modeled_behavior: Entered by Human Basic Stage 4 or Mech Basic Stage 4.
Lasts 5 seconds.
Enables Seraphic Duet when Synchronization Rate is at least 100.

states.name: Stardust Resonance
modeled_behavior: Granted by Overdrive.
Tracked for implemented Aemeath-lite routing; extra unimplemented utility remains omitted.

states.name: Forte Enhancement
modeled_behavior: Granted by Overdrive / 大招1 as 2 stacks for 30 seconds.
Seraphic Duet consumes 1 stack when available and selects the enhanced 10-hit Tune Rupture follow-up.
This state is the source-backed enhanced follow-up selector; Stardust Resonance itself is not the selector.

states.name: Trail No-cost
modeled_behavior: Granted by Overdrive / 大招1 for 30 seconds.
Overdrive grants one preservation use for the next actual Seraphic Duet.
The first actual Seraphic Duet while preservation is active snapshots target Rupturous Trail stacks, preserves the target stacks, then consumes preservation.
Enhanced-use charges, preservation state, and target Rupturous Trail stacks are separate state concepts.

states.name: Heavenfall Edict: Unbound
modeled_behavior: Granted by Overdrive.
Enables Finale condition tracking.

states.name: Instant Response
modeled_behavior: Active when Unbound is active and Resonance Rate reaches 4.
Makes Charged II use Charged I timing.
Charged II during Instant Response recovers Synchronization Rate through the implemented instant_response_sync_delta behavior.
Consumed or cleared after applicable Charged II / Finale behavior according to current code.

states.name: Aemeath Resonance Mode
modeled_behavior: Configured as fusion_burst, tune_rupture, or unresolved.
fusion_burst emits fusion_burst on supported damage trigger actions.
tune_rupture emits tune_rupture_shifting on supported damage trigger actions.
unresolved emits no event and logs the unresolved fallback.

known_runtime_modes.name: Aemeath Resonance Mode
known_runtime_modes.notes: Party presets or UI overrides can select the mode.

forte_circuit.title: Aemeath Forte Circuit
modeled_behavior: The simulator can request generated mechanic damage packets from character mechanics after normal action damage is resolved.
Generated damage packets preserve source_action_id, source_status, source_ref, source_rows, formula_type, repeat_count, and notes.
Packets only add runtime damage when the config has source-confirmed formula type, coefficient, scaling, and repeat count.
Aemeath Seraphic Duet Tune Rupture follow-up damage is source-confirmed from action rows 2786/2787/2931/2932 and dmg rows 2578/2579/2628/2629.
The source multiplier is 1.0935 and uses the existing tune_response base-10000 formula path.
exclusions: No coefficient is guessed from external simulator sites.
No reward shaping, PPO policy action, rotation forcing, or profile stat changes are part of this implementation.

rupturous_trail.title: Rupturous Trail
modeled_behavior: Rupturous Trail is a source-confirmed C0 Tune Rupture target mechanic.
Each successfully executed party Tune Rupture response damage event grants 10 target stacks.
A response blocked by its response cooldown grants no stacks.
C0 maximum is 30 stacks.
Duration is 30 combat seconds and uses the combat_time clock.
Each successful application refreshes the aggregate target duration to 30 combat seconds.
Seraphic Duet snapshots the target stack count when committed.
Each stack increases the extra Tune Rupture multiplier by 4%.
Normal Seraphic Duet uses 5 repeats; Enhanced Seraphic Duet uses 10 repeats.
Overdrive grants two enhanced-use charges and one preservation use.
The first actual Seraphic Duet while preservation is active snapshots and preserves target stacks, then consumes preservation.
The next actual Seraphic Duet snapshots and removes the target stacks.
Enhanced-use charges, preservation state, and target trail stacks are separate state concepts.
Observation schema v5 exposes target stack and remaining ratios as fixed global channels.
Legacy character_mechanics_state["aemeath"] personal Rupturous Trail keys are ignored and removed during Aemeath state normalization.
rupturous_trail.duration_refresh_rule: refresh aggregate target duration to 30 combat seconds on each successful application

fusion_trail.title: Fusion Trail
modeled_behavior: State is clamped to its configured max stack count.
Remaining duration ticks with action time and clears stacks when it reaches zero.
Fusion Burst explosion damage is not applied without source-confirmed coefficient/formula data.

seraphic_duet_followup_damage.title: Seraphic Duet Follow-up Damage
modeled_behavior: Seraphic Duet asks the Forte Circuit config for follow-up packets in tune_rupture mode.
Normal follow-up uses 强化E-震谐: 1.0935 x 5 tune_response hits.
Enhanced follow-up uses 强化E-震谐增幅: 1.0935 x 10 tune_response hits.
Enhanced follow-up is selected by forte_enhancement_stacks from 大招1 / 强化E增幅次数, not directly by Stardust Resonance.
Interfered Marker +40% remains target damage-taken amp through the formula path and is not baked into the 1.0935 source coefficient.
1.531 / 1.5309 is not hardcoded as a coefficient.

stardust_resonance_interaction.title: Stardust Resonance Interaction
modeled_behavior: Stardust Resonance remains tracked as an Aemeath state.
Observation channel mechanic_state_3 now represents Heavenfall Unbound or Stardust Resonance activity.
The Seraphic Duet follow-up log records whether Stardust Resonance was active, but Stardust Resonance itself does not select the 10-hit enhanced follow-up.
The direct 10-hit selector is forte_enhancement_stacks / 强化E增幅次数.

generated_mechanic_damage.title: Generated Mechanic Damage
modeled_behavior: Character mechanics can return generated damage packets without adding policy-selectable actions.
Runtime-applicable packets contribute to total damage, hit details, hit counts, timeline entries, and damage logs.
Unresolved packets remain visible in generated_mechanic_damage_events but add zero damage and zero generated hits.
Normal generated damage is explicitly blocked until damage bonus routing is source-safe; current source-confirmed Aemeath follow-up uses tune_response.

source_gated_implementation.title: Source-gated Implementation
rules: Runtime generated damage requires traceable coefficient/formula/repeat/stack/duration data from source spreadsheets, extracted Aemeath audit files, or generated reports.
Seraphic Duet Tune Rupture follow-up satisfies this rule with action rows 2786/2787/2931/2932 and dmg rows 2578/2579/2628/2629.
Rows with visible event timing or labels but no usable coefficient/formula remain metadata only.
External simulator websites are intentionally not used as authority for runtime damage.
Build profile stats, weapon static stats, PPO training, reward shaping, and policy action spaces are not changed by this Forte Circuit patch.

remaining_unresolved_mechanics: Aemeath S1 kill trajectory transfer, Aemeath S2 kill-triggered detonation, and Aemeath S5 all-effects behavior.
Exact hit-level timing, hitstop, enemy movement, hit loss, and multi-target trail state tracking.

action_resolution_priority.rule: Seraphic Duet if Seraphic Duo is active and Synchronization Rate is at least 100
Sync Strike if a sync-strike window is active
Special Overdrive E1 form switch if the Overdrive follow-up window is active

timing_model.name: action_time
timing_model.notes: Action lock, internal action progression, hit timing progression, buffs, anomalies, and mechanic state duration ticking.

timing_model.name: combat_time_cost
timing_model.notes: Timed combat clock cost and cooldown reduction time. If omitted from an action, it falls back to action_time.

timing_model.name: Overdrive
timing_model.notes: Confirmed global time-stop style action in current source interpretation.

timing_model.name: Finale
timing_model.notes: Confirmed global time-stop style action in current source interpretation.

form_switch: Normal Human -> Mech Form Switch immediately casts Mech Basic Stage 1.
Normal Mech -> Human Form Switch immediately casts Human Basic Stage 1.
Both normal form switches share a 1s form-switch cooldown.
Special post-Overdrive E1 switch to Human immediately casts Human Basic Stage 2.
Normal Form Switch sets the next opposite-form Basic stage appropriately.
heavy_attack: Outside Instant Response, the policy action resolves to Charged I in the current form.
During Instant Response, the policy action resolves to Charged II in the current form.
Instant Response Charged II uses the corresponding Charged I timing override.
Heavy Charged source damage type maps to resonance_liberation damage_bonus_category in current action data.

sync_strike.name: Armament Merge
modeled_behavior: Human-side Sync Strike.
Switches to Mech.
Sets the next Mech Basic Attack to Stage 2.
Can emit the selected Aemeath Resonance Mode event when it deals damage.

sync_strike.name: Call of Dawn
modeled_behavior: Mech-side Sync Strike.
Switches to Human.
Sets the next Human Basic Attack to Stage 2.
Can emit the selected Aemeath Resonance Mode event when it deals damage.

seraphic_duet.name: Overturn/Ouverture
modeled_behavior: Human-side Seraphic Duet.
Requires Seraphic Duo and Synchronization Rate at least 100.
Costs 100 Synchronization Rate.
Adds Resonance Rate.
Switches to Mech.
Uses resonance_liberation damage_bonus_category from source damage type.
In Tune Rupture mode, source-confirmed Seraphic Duet follow-up damage snapshots target Rupturous Trail stacks and applies the C0 4% per-stack extra Tune Rupture multiplier.

seraphic_duet.name: Encore
modeled_behavior: Mech-side Seraphic Duet.
Requires Seraphic Duo and Synchronization Rate at least 100.
Costs 100 Synchronization Rate.
Adds Resonance Rate.
Switches to Human.
Uses resonance_liberation damage_bonus_category from source damage type.
In Tune Rupture mode, source-confirmed Seraphic Duet follow-up damage snapshots target Rupturous Trail stacks and applies the C0 4% per-stack extra Tune Rupture multiplier.

seraphic_duet.name: Priority
modeled_behavior: Seraphic Duet takes priority over normal Form Switch.

overdrive_finale.name: Overdrive
modeled_behavior: Grants Unbound.
Grants Stardust Resonance in the current implementation.
Adds 30 Synchronization Rate.
Adds 1 Resonance Rate.
Grants 2 Forte Enhancement stacks for 30 seconds.
Grants Trail No-cost for 30 seconds.
Switches to Mech.
Opens the special E1 follow-up window.
Costs 0 timed combat seconds.

overdrive_finale.name: Finale
modeled_behavior: Requires Unbound, Synchronization Rate 200, and Resonance Rate 4.
Clears Synchronization Rate and Resonance Rate.
Ends Unbound and Seraphic Duo.
Switches to Human.
Costs 0 timed combat seconds.

echo_sets.name: Trailblazing Star 5-set
echo_sets.timing: same-action aggregate approximation for triggering damage
echo_sets.notes: The 2-set Fusion DMG bonus is treated as already reflected in the selected Aemeath real profile.

weapons.name: Everbright Polestar

runtime_effects.name: Resonance Liberation Penetration
runtime_effects.trigger_description: Tune Rupture - Shifting / Fusion Burst triggers an 8s resonance_liberation penetration buff.
runtime_effects.notes: DEF Ignore and Fusion RES Ignore are formula modifiers, not damage bonuses.

exclusions: not applied to Tune Break formula damage
not applied to Tune Response formula damage

tune_break.name: Aemeath Tune Break
tune_break.availability: Conditional on enemy Mistune / Tune Break availability.
tune_break.notes: Uses Tune formula damage, not normal damage bonus or Everbright conditional penetration.

response_damage.name: Tune Rupture Response - Starburst
response_damage.notes: Off-field party response; not affected by Everbright conditional penetration.

qte_intro_outro.name: Aemeath QTE / Intro transition metadata
qte_intro_outro.notes: Reviewed QTE/Intro candidates and transition actions exist. This is not listed as fully excluded.

sync_delta_table.name: Form Switch to Aemeath After Overdrive

resonance_mode_mechanic_events.title: Aemeath Resonance Mode Mechanic Events
mode_behavior: fusion_burst mode emits event tag fusion_burst when a listed trigger action deals damage.
tune_rupture mode emits event tag tune_rupture_shifting when a listed trigger action deals damage.
unresolved mode emits no event and logs aemeath_resonance_mode_unresolved_no_events_emit.
resonance_mode_mechanic_events.cooldown_rule: The same skill can trigger once every 3 seconds. Multi-hit actions emit at most one event per action execution.

damage_bonus_category_source_damage_type.title: Damage Bonus Category / Source Damage Type
modeled_behavior: action_type and damage_bonus_category are intentionally separate fields.
action_type controls mechanical routing, cooldown grouping, policy identity, and character mechanics.
damage_bonus_category controls additive DMG Bonus lookup from build profiles.
Seraphic Duet and Heavy Charged actions use resonance_liberation damage bonus from source damage type.
Overdrive and Finale are Resonance Liberation actions and use Resonance Liberation damage bonus.

build_profile_damage_type_bonus_notes.title: Build Profile / Damage Type Bonus Notes

profiles.default: Backward-compatible default profile derived from existing characters.json stats.
profiles.liberation_focus_test: Editable test build assumption that adds Resonance Liberation DMG Bonus through data/build_profiles.json. It is intended to test Liberation-heavy damage distribution and is not claimed as final real-game build data.
profiles.aemeath_user_real_01: User-supplied profile with Trailblazing Star 2-set and Everbright Polestar static stats already reflected where marked.

build_profile_damage_type_bonus_notes.notes: Build profiles are stat overlays only. They do not change Aemeath action routing, coefficients, timings, resources, or policy action availability.

known_limitations: This page describes the simulator's modeled interpretation, not a complete game-client recreation.
The current goal is DPS comparison, not exact frame-perfect combat reproduction.
Fusion Burst explosion runtime damage remains omitted until coefficient/formula data is source-confirmed.
Fusion Trail stack state remains scaffolded until source-confirmed stack mutation and damage rules are available.
Seraphic Duet Tune Rupture follow-up damage is implemented only for the source-confirmed tune_response formula; Fusion Burst follow-up damage remains omitted.
exact hitstop, enemy movement, hit loss, and multi-target state tracking remain omitted.
exact Tune Strain stack system remains omitted.
aerial and dodge-counter actions remain omitted unless later represented in action data.
simplified_assumptions: Trailblazing Star 5-set uses same-action aggregate approximation for triggering damage.
Tune Break and Tune Response are modeled as single-target formula damage.
QTE / Intro transition data is represented through reviewed metadata and transition actions, not a full cinematic/client transition recreation.
Aemeath Rupturous Trail is modeled as a single-target CombatState trail state rather than full multi-target client state.
Observation schema slot_generic_mechanics_v5 exposes target Rupturous Trail as fixed global channels.
